title: Overflow Detection in Fixed-point Arithmetic
summary: Defines overflow, explains sign extension for changing representation width, and derives the C2 overflow detection rule using carry bits and NXOR.
content_points: Overflow: result of an operation falls outside [min, max] of the representation.
Sign extension to n+k bits: A+S – insert k zeros after sign bit; C1/C2 – repeat sign bit k times.
C2 overflow condition: carry-out C_n ≠ carry into sign position C_{n-1}. Detected by NXOR gate.
Overflow cannot occur when adding numbers of opposite signs.
C2 Theorem 2: if no overflow, the result fits in n bits (take lower n bits of formal sum).

formulas.name: C2 overflow test
formulas.expression: overflow = C_{n-1} XOR C_n  (1 → overflow occurred)

learning_objectives: Define overflow for a given fixed-point representation
Apply sign extension to change representation width
Detect overflow in C2 using the carry-bit rule
